original_query: select c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice, sum(l_quantity) from customer, orders, lineitem where o_orderkey in ( select l_orderkey from lineitem group by l_orderkey having sum(l_quantity) > 315 ) and c_custkey = o_custkey and o_orderkey = l_orderkey group by c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice order by o_totalprice desc, o_orderdate limit 100;
rewritten_query:
SELECT 
    c.c_name, 
    c.c_custkey, 
    o.o_orderkey, 
    o.o_orderdate, 
    o.o_totalprice, 
    t.total_qty
FROM customer c
JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey
JOIN (
    SELECT l_orderkey, SUM(l_quantity) AS total_qty
    FROM lineitem
    GROUP BY l_orderkey
    HAVING SUM(l_quantity) > 315
) t ON o.o_orderkey = t.l_orderkey
ORDER BY o.o_totalprice DESC, o.o_orderdate
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Convert the IN subquery to a JOIN with a derived table, which eliminates the need to scan the lineitem table twice
Eliminate redundant aggregation by reusing the sum already calculated in the subquery instead of computing it again in the main query
Use explicit JOIN syntax instead of implicit joins in the WHERE clause for better readability and optimization

original_query: select c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice, sum(l_quantity) from customer, orders, lineitem where o_orderkey in ( select l_orderkey from lineitem group by l_orderkey having sum(l_quantity) > 313 ) and c_custkey = o_custkey and o_orderkey = l_orderkey group by c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice order by o_totalprice desc, o_orderdate limit 100;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_orders AS (
    SELECT 
        o.o_orderkey,
        o.o_custkey,
        o.o_orderdate,
        o.o_totalprice,
        sq.total_qty
    FROM 
        orders o
        JOIN (
            SELECT 
                l_orderkey, 
                SUM(l_quantity) AS total_qty
            FROM 
                lineitem
            GROUP BY 
                l_orderkey
            HAVING 
                SUM(l_quantity) > 313
        ) sq ON o.o_orderkey = sq.l_orderkey
)
SELECT
    c.c_name,
    c.c_custkey,
    fo.o_orderkey,
    fo.o_orderdate,
    fo.o_totalprice,
    fo.total_qty
FROM
    customer c
    JOIN filtered_orders fo ON c.c_custkey = fo.o_custkey
ORDER BY
    fo.o_totalprice DESC,
    fo.o_orderdate
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Eliminated redundant GROUP BY in the main query by recognizing that each o_orderkey is unique in the subquery result
Converted the IN subquery to a JOIN and included the sum in the subquery's result to avoid recalculating it
Eliminated the need to join with the lineitem table twice by reusing the computed sum from the subquery

original_query: select c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice, sum(l_quantity) from customer, orders, lineitem where o_orderkey in ( select l_orderkey from lineitem group by l_orderkey having sum(l_quantity) > 313 ) and c_custkey = o_custkey and o_orderkey = l_orderkey group by c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice order by o_totalprice desc, o_orderdate limit 100;
rewritten_query:
WITH order_sums AS (
  SELECT l_orderkey, SUM(l_quantity) AS total_quantity
  FROM lineitem
  GROUP BY l_orderkey
  HAVING SUM(l_quantity) > 313
)
SELECT c.c_name, c.c_custkey, o.o_orderkey, o.o_orderdate, o.o_totalprice, os.total_quantity
FROM customer c
JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey
JOIN order_sums os ON o.o_orderkey = os.l_orderkey
ORDER BY o.o_totalprice DESC, o.o_orderdate
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Replace the IN subquery with a JOIN to a CTE that precomputes the sum of lineitem quantities
Eliminate redundant GROUP BY and SUM operations in the main query by reusing the precomputed sum from the CTE
Use explicit JOIN syntax instead of implicit joins in the WHERE clause for better readability and optimization
